field.note: newly surfaced; not deep-profiled — Rust, local ONNX embeddings, SQLite-backed semantic search

field_note: Direct comparators unchanged since 2026-07-10 (same-day prior scan); light re-scan surfaced additional small local-first peers (edg-l/engram-mcp, Memorized, memory-mcp, Vestige, CaliLuke/rusty-mcp) — all ship local embeddings + SQLite, reinforcing those as cluster table-stakes. Field not re-saturated.

matrix.evidence: src/model_embed.rs (all-MiniLM-L6-v2 via candle) + weighted fusion; benches/recall.rs R@5 0.731

matrix.note: DELIBERATE design trade-off (ADR-0024), not a deficiency: engram re-encrypts the whole store per write so `forget` is a TRUE immediate physical delete — an append-only log defers physical deletion to compaction. With the Argon2id key cached, the whole-store write is cheap at scale (measured: ~1ms at 10k, ~11ms at 100k; benches/persist.rs). Peers' SQLite/WAL writes are incremental but do not physically delete in place. Revisit if save latency at a realistic N becomes a problem, or the hard-delete guarantee is relaxed.

since_last_scan.field: core direct comparators unchanged; several small local-first peers newly surfaced

notes: engram moved BEHIND -> COMPETITIVE by closing the two gaps that traced to one root (no semantic embedder): a real in-process embedder + dense-weighted fusion (+34.6pt R@5 measured) and a reproducible recall benchmark. The one remaining table-stakes gap is genuinely-incremental persistence; its dominant cost is already gone. engram still leads the whole cluster on a structurally-enforced egress privacy tier and mutation-proven correctness — no peer has either.
